text:
There was once a girl named Olivia who had the curiosity of a cat. Every morning she would trot down to the creek to watch the sunrise over the water lilies. She had an uncanny ability to listen into conversations without being noticed.

One day as the golden light painted the sky, a wise old owl fluttered down onto a branch beside her. "Olivia," the owl said calmly, "I've seen you eavesdrop. What gets you so eager?"

Olivia felt a blush warm her cheeks. "Nothing much," she replied. "Just listening to people talk."

"You're wise to listen, Olivia," the owl continued. "Every word they say has its story. Sometimes we're the audience to things."

After some thought, Olivia realized the wisdom in that. She decided then to listen more attentively.

One afternoon, a young girl came down the path. She had lost her puppy, Scout. Olivia approached with kind eyes.

"Boo! I'm here!"
